name: Hub
function: Broadcasts incoming electrical signals to all connected ports
description: A basic Layer 1 network device that repeats incoming signals to all other ports regardless of destination. Creates a single collision domain and is largely obsolete, replaced by switches.

name: Repeater
function: Amplifies and retransmits signals to extend network reach
description: A Layer 1 device that regenerates and retransmits network signals to extend the distance they can travel without degradation. Used to overcome cable length limitations.

name: Bridge
function: Connects two network segments and forwards frames based on MAC addresses
description: A Layer 2 device that divides a network into segments, reducing collision domains. It learns MAC addresses and forwards frames only to the appropriate segment, improving efficiency.

name: Layer 2 Switch
function: Forwards frames between devices using MAC address tables
description: A Layer 2 device that creates a dedicated connection between sender and receiver by learning MAC addresses. Each port is its own collision domain, providing much better performance than hubs.

name: Layer 3 Switch
function: Performs both Layer 2 switching and Layer 3 routing in hardware
description: A switch with built-in routing capability that can forward packets between VLANs and subnets using hardware-accelerated routing tables, combining the speed of a switch with the intelligence of a router.

name: Router
function: Forwards packets between networks using IP routing tables
description: A Layer 3 device that connects multiple networks and determines the best path for packet forwarding using routing protocols. The fundamental building block of inter-network connectivity.

name: Firewall
function: Filters network traffic based on security rules at L3/L4 (and up to L7 for NGFW)
description: A security device that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predefined rules. Stateful firewalls track connection state; next-generation firewalls (NGFW) also perform deep packet inspection up to Layer 7.

name: Wireless Access Point
function: Connects wireless clients to a wired network using radio frequencies
description: A Layer 2 device that creates a wireless local area network (WLAN) and bridges wireless clients to the wired Ethernet infrastructure. Acts as a central transmitter and receiver of wireless signals.

name: Load Balancer
function: Distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers to ensure availability and performance
description: A device or software that distributes network or application traffic across multiple backend servers. Layer 4 load balancers work on TCP/UDP connections; Layer 7 load balancers can route based on HTTP content, cookies, and URLs.

name: Proxy Server
function: Forwards client requests to external servers on behalf of clients
description: A Layer 7 intermediary that receives client requests and forwards them to destination servers. Provides caching, content filtering, anonymisation, and access control. A forward proxy sits between clients and the internet.

name: Reverse Proxy
function: Sits in front of servers and forwards client requests to appropriate backend services
description: A Layer 7 intermediary that accepts requests from clients and forwards them to backend servers, hiding the internal network topology. Provides TLS termination, caching, compression, and load balancing.

name: VPN Gateway
function: Creates encrypted tunnels between remote networks or clients over the internet
description: A Layer 3 device that establishes secure Virtual Private Network tunnels using encryption protocols. Allows remote users or branch offices to securely access private networks over public infrastructure.

name: IDS
function: Monitors network traffic to detect and alert on suspicious activity
description: An Intrusion Detection System passively analyses network traffic or host logs to detect known attack signatures and anomalies. It generates alerts but does not block traffic — a key distinction from IPS.

name: IPS
function: Monitors and actively blocks malicious network traffic in real time
description: An Intrusion Prevention System sits inline in the network path and actively drops or rejects malicious packets and connections. Builds on IDS capabilities by adding automated blocking responses.

name: Modem
function: Converts digital data to analogue signals and vice versa for transmission over phone or cable lines
description: A Layer 1 device (modulator-demodulator) that converts between digital network signals and analogue signals suitable for transmission over telephone lines, cable TV infrastructure, or fibre optic networks.
